title: Yamal fit to start on bench for Spain FIFA World Cup opener against Cape Verde
original_text: Yamal fit to start on bench for Spain FIFA World Cup opener against Cape Verde left Spain's Lamine Yamal during training. REUTERS https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/11lfwx/article71102557.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/2026-06-14T150434Z_1014948609_UP1EM6E15VLAS_RTRMADP_3_SOCCER-WORLDCUP-ESP-CPV-PREVIEW.JPG CLAUDIA GRECO Soccer Football - FIFA World Cup 2026 - Spain Training - Kennesaw State University, Kennesaw, Georgia, U.S. - June 14, 2026 Spain's Lamine Yamal during training REUTERS/Claudia Greco Yamal has not played since April, as the 18-year-old struggled ‚Äåwith groin issues and a hamstring injury. ATLANTA Spain winger ‚ÄãLamine Yamal is fit and ready to come ‚ÅÝoff the bench in his side‚Äôs opening World Cup game, but manager Luis de la Fuente said on Sunday that his entrance will depend on ‚Äåhow the Group H match with Cape Verde progresses. Yamal has not played since April, as the 18-year-old struggled ‚Äåwith groin issues and a hamstring injury. He missed ‚ÄåSpain‚Äôs ‚ÅÝrecent friendlies but his coach has no concerns over ‚ÅÝhis fitness, and the situation is the same for Nico Williams and Victor Munoz. ‚ÄúThe good news is that Lamine is very fit,‚Äù De la Fuente told ‚Äãreporters. ‚ÄúHe‚Äôs following the set process, ‚Äåand he‚Äôs in good shape, he‚Äôs training very well, just like his other colleagues who had some sort of injury. They‚Äôre all available, and they won‚Äôt be able to play ‚Äåfrom the start of the match. ‚ÄúWe‚Äôll have to wait and ‚Äãsee how the game progresses, and then we‚Äôll make a decision on how they can contribute to ‚ÅÝthe team. ‚ÄúIt‚Äôs a matter of logic, of how the game is evolving, the game situation.‚Äù SPAIN AMONG FAVOURITES Spain, European champion and World Cup ‚Äåwinner in 2010, goes into the tournament as one of the strong favourites to lift the trophy, but De la Fuente will not be taking Cape Verde for granted on its World Cup debut. ‚ÄúIf you haven‚Äôt watched them play yet, you‚Äôll see tomorrow,‚Äù he said. ‚ÄúTactically they are a very well-structured team and they ‚Äådevelop those tactical concepts very well. Their players are very powerful from ‚Äãa physical point of view. They‚Äôre very fast and they knocked out Cameroon. ‚ÄúThey might be one of the ‚ÅÝteams springing a surprise in this World Cup and the match tomorrow ‚ÅÝwill require us to perform to our best and it will be a struggle for sure, even if ‚Äåwe win. The public, everyone will discover tomorrow what they can do.‚Äù Saudi Arabia and Uruguay are the other teams ‚Äãin the group and they meet in Miami on Monday.
